The basal ganglia, particularly the putamen, are frequently affected because these areas have vessels that are more prone to aneurysmal dilation under high blood pressure conditions. These weak points are more prevalent in deeper brain regions like the basal ganglia, leading to bleeding there rather than other areas. Hence, I believe the correct answer is A: Basal ganglia.
